subject: Psychology
grade_subject_fit: Source-derived Cycle A learning package using teacher/student separation, fictional examples, tiered major work, decision plus reason, and evidence-based explanation.
unit_context: Cycle A A4 explores how learning shapes behaviour through association, consequences, observation, practice, and context.
assignment_purpose: Students explain behaviour change using learning concepts while avoiding blame, labels, or one-cause explanations.
final_evidence_target: A learning case analysis that identifies the learning process, evidence, decision plus reason, alternative explanation, and limit.

student_task_flow.label: Learning concepts
student_task_flow.evidence: Students define classical conditioning, operant conditioning, reinforcement, punishment, modelling, extinction, generalization, and shaping.

student_task_flow.label: Fictional learning cases
student_task_flow.evidence: Students match evidence to learning concepts.

student_task_flow.label: Behaviour change plan
student_task_flow.evidence: Students choose a learning-informed support and justify it.

success_criteria: I can identify a learning process from evidence.
I can distinguish reinforcement and punishment.
I can make a decision and give a reason.
I can include a limit or alternative explanation.
differentiation_model: Supported students use labelled examples. Proficient students choose and justify the best learning concept. Extending students design a support plan and explain why it avoids blame or coercion.
checkpoint_release_logic: Students complete vocabulary and one evidence table before final case analysis.
teacher_implementation_notes: Avoid turning behaviour into character labels.
Keep support plans humane, practical, and school-appropriate.
likely_misconceptions: Punishment and negative reinforcement mean the same thing.
One event proves the learning process.
pacing_shape: Five-part package: vocabulary, conditioning, consequences, observational learning, final support design.

teacher_guide.title: Cycle A A4 Teacher Guide — Learning and Behaviour
teacher_guide.overview: Students learn how behaviour changes through association, consequences, observation, and practice. The package keeps examples fictional and focuses on support rather than blame.
teacher_guide.essential_question: How do people learn behaviours, and how can we explain behaviour change carefully?
teacher_guide.big_idea: Learning explanations are strongest when they connect a process to evidence and include context and limits.
teacher_guide.opening_frame: Learning is not about blaming people. It is about noticing patterns, consequences, models, and environments that shape behaviour.
teacher_guide.frame_line: A4 frame: behaviour, evidence, learning process, support, limit.
teacher_guide.assessment_focus: Final work should identify a learning process, explain evidence, choose a support, and state a limit.
integrity_checks: No public behaviour ranking of classmates.
teacher_notes: When students say someone is lazy, ask what pattern, consequence, or context could be involved.
When students mix up negative reinforcement and punishment, return to whether behaviour increases or decreases.
For extension, students compare two possible interventions.
questions: What support would be fair and useful?

model.title: Learning case model
steps: Behaviour: a character avoids a task after repeated embarrassment.
Evidence: avoidance increases after the event.
Learning process: association and consequences may both matter.
Support: smaller practice steps and predictable feedback.
Limit: the case does not prove only one cause.

content.scenario: A fictional student stops volunteering after a negative classroom experience.

content.model: Support should be humane, specific, and connected to evidence.

task_sheet.title: A4 Student Packet — Learning and Behaviour
task_sheet.purpose_line: Use fictional cases to explain behaviour change with learning concepts.
instructions: Make a decision and give a reason.

tasks.label: Vocabulary organizer
tasks.prompt: Define classical conditioning, operant conditioning, reinforcement, punishment, modelling, extinction, generalization, and shaping.

tasks.label: Supported pathway
tasks.prompt: Use the case table to match behaviour, evidence, and learning process.

tasks.label: Proficient pathway
tasks.prompt: Choose the best learning explanation for the case. Give evidence and a limit.

tasks.label: Extending pathway
tasks.prompt: Design a humane support plan and explain why it fits the learning evidence.

tasks.label: Final assessment
tasks.prompt: Analyze a fictional learning case using behaviour, evidence, process, support, and limit.

success_criteria: I included a support and limit.

exit_ticket.title: A4 Exit Ticket
exit_ticket.prompt: Name one learning process that fits a fictional case and explain one limit.
